summary:
There is no evidence within the provided sources linking tivantinib to efficacy in MET-high hepatocellar carcinoma. Despite its mechanistic plausibility as an anti-MET therapy, documented studies evaluating it in this patient population were absent in the review material.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$

Reasoning Summary: 
Tivantinib has not been evaluated in MET-high hepatocellular carcinoma according to the cited literature; therefore, its effectiveness remains unproven in this setting due to complete absence of supportive clinical trial data.

All these point toward total absence of evidentiary basis for claiming benefit in MET-high HCC via existing records.

✅ Sentence 2: Due to the absence of reported clinical study results involving tivantinib in MET-high HCC, there is currently no indication of its efficacy in this subgroup.
